data.title: Britain's DFS Furniture forecasts 2025 profit above expectations
data.description:
DFS Furniture said on Thursday it expects to report annual profit above market expectations, driven by robust sales, effective cost management and the assumption that there would be no further supply chain disruptions.

The company, which has been grappling with rising costs in a volatile economy, has taken to actively cutting expenses.

A government mandated increase in employer social security contributions and minimum wages introduced in the UK budget in October add pressure on the retailer's costs.

Still, the company said it expects its 2025 profit before tax and brand amortisation to be between 25 million pounds and 29 million pounds ($32.38 million and $37.56 million), ahead of a forecast of 22.7 million pounds by analysts in a company-compiled poll.

DFS has been streamlining and simplifying its operations, and is targeting 50 million pounds in annualised cost savings by 2026.

"Our confidence in the Group's capabilities and future potential has never been higher," CEO Tim Stacey said.

Stacey added that the firm was on track to achieve its 1.4 billion pound ($1.81 billion) revenue target for the full year.

($1 = 0.7720 pounds)

data.title: Roche's Zealand Pharma Deal Signals Commitment to Obesity Drug Market — Market Talk
data.description: Faster price erosion and high rebates by insurers in the U.S. obesity market could overshadow Roche's long-term opportunities there, Morgan Stanley analysts say in a note. The Zealand Pharma deal to co-develop a weight-loss therapy with petrelintide allows the Swiss pharma giant to access a differentiated and current best-in-class obesity treatment and signals a commitment to the segment, they say. Morgan Stanley analysts expect petrelintide peak sales of 7 billion Swiss francs, which could support a consensus upgrade by 4%. Recent share-value increase suggest that the market has already priced in this. (helena.smolak@wsj.com)

data.title: Paguld Intelligent Manufacturing Signs Cooperation Deal With Hydraulic Power Machinery Manufacturer
data.description:
Qingdao Paguld Intelligent Manufacturing signed a cooperation agreement with hydraulic power machinery manufacturer Shandong Wanto Hydraulic, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

Under the five-year deal, Wanto Hydraulic will be allowed to subscribe to the Chinese centralized lubrication equipment manufacturer's convertible bonds or shares should the latter do so.

Both parties will also regard each other as important business partners in the procurement of hydraulic products and related components, the disclosure said.

data.title: Jubilant Pharmova Unit's Solid Oral Formulations Plant Gets VAI Status from US FDA
data.description:
Jubilant Pharmova said its subsidiary, Jubilant Cadista Pharmaceuticals, received an establishment inspection report with voluntary action indicated status from the US Food and Drug Administration for its solid oral formulations facility in Maryland, US.

The inspection, conducted in January, is now closed. However, the facility will not manufacture any products as its operations have been closed, according to an Indian bourse filing on Wednesday.

data.title: China Stocks Fall for Second Session
data.description:
The Shanghai Composite fell 0.39% to close at 3,359, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.99% to 10,736 on Thursday, marking a second consecutive session of declines, with technology and AI-related stocks leading the retreat.

UBS downgraded China’s technology sector from attractive to neutral, citing strong year-to-date gains of over 30% and concerns over future growth prospects.

Analysts have also raised concerns about China’s ability to meet its economic targets, as outlined in the recently concluded Two Sessions annual meetings.

Additionally, escalating trade tensions weighed on sentiment after US President Donald Trump’s steel and aluminum tariffs took effect on Wednesday.

Losses in the tech sector were led by Shanghai Stonehill (-6.8%), Talkweb Information (-1.2%), ZTE Corp (-4.2%), Inesa Intelligent Tech (-6.8%) and Cambricon Technologies (-4.1%).

data.title: Wetteri Q4 Adjusted EBITDA EUR 3.6 Mln
data.description:
Wetteri Oyj :

  • Q4 GROUP'S REVENUE WAS EUR 116.8 MILLION (EUR 131.1 MILLION)

  • Q4 ADJUSTED EBITDA WAS EUR 3.6 MILLION (EUR 6.8 MILLION)

data.title: CVR Energy Insider Bought Shares Worth $5,335,243, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Carl C Icahn, 10% Owner, on March 10, 2025, executed a purchase for 295,221 shares in CVR Energy for $5,335,243. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Icahn has control over a total of 68,446,293 common shares of the company, with 68,446,293 controlled indirectly.

SEC Filing:

www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1376139/000106299325005273/xslF345X05/form4.xml

data.description:

  • DAR GLOBAL PLC - FY PROFIT BEFORE TAX USD 14.1 MILLION
